sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Grace Ross, is a 24-year-old female presenting with symptoms of discomfort, change in discharge, and odor in her vaginal region.
She reports that the right side of her vagina has become painful.
She denies any fevers or chills.
She has recently learned that her partner has been engaging in sexual encounters with other people, which has prompted her to seek evaluation for all STDs.
She reports a change in vaginal discharge, which smells different and has a different consistency than usual, and has been experiencing vaginal pain on the right side for several days.
She also mentions that her partner has been cheating on her for approximately 3 months.
She has a history of an irregular Pap smear at the age of 20, but has not had a Pap smear since then.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of an irregular Pap smear at the age of 20, which has not been repeated since.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms other than those mentioned.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The pelvic exam demonstrates no external lesions, normal labia majora and minora, normal physiological discharge with normal color and smell.
The cervix is normal, vaginal wall is normal, no cervical motion tenderness, and no adnexal tenderness or masses noted.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is at high risk for STDs due to an abnormal Pap smear in the past and her partner's history of multiple partners.
Problem list: Chlamydia; Gonorrhea; Trichomonas; Syphilis; HIV; Hepatitis C; Abnormal Pap smear; High risk for STDs.
Plan:
I discussed the plan with the patient to perform STD testing for gonorrhea, chlamydia, trichomonas, and obtain blood samples for syphilis, HIV, and hepatitis C.
The patient will be notified if any abnormal results are found, and if not, she can call the office in 3 days for results.
I prescribed [NOT MENTIONED].
Follow-up: The patient will be seen at her next annual checkup if all results are normal.
